Monsoon.impact: Riverine flooding in Indus and Chenab basins, urban flooding in Lahore/Rawalpindi.
institutional: Reinforce embankments along the Chenab and Indus rivers.
Establish relief camps and pre-position motorboats and emergency supplies.
Activate early warning systems at district levels.
household: Elevate electronic appliances and food reserves above historical flood levels.
Identify nearest designated high-ground community shelter.
Store clean drinking water and emergency medical kits.

Winter.impact: Dense fog and mild temperature drops, potential dry spell leading to smog.
institutional: Coordinate highway traffic control for low visibility periods.
Monitor air quality index and issue health advisories.
household: Use warm clothing and minimize outdoor exposure during peak smog hours.
Keep emergency contact numbers for highway police handy.

SpringSummer.impact: Heatwaves and sudden pre-monsoon windstorms causing crop damage.
institutional: Set up heatwave response centers and public cooling stations.
Issue irrigation schedules to match forecasted water availability.
household: Avoid direct exposure to sunlight during peak hours (11 AM to 4 PM).
Keep livestock hydrated and sheltered from extreme heat.

Monsoon.impact: Flash floods in hill torrents of Kirthar range, severe urban inundation in Karachi.
institutional: De-silt major drainage channels and storm-water drains in Karachi.
Evacuate vulnerable populations in low-lying coastal areas.
Pre-position relief resources at the divisional level.
household: Avoid contact with raw sewage and flooded roads to prevent waterborne diseases.
Turn off main gas and electrical switches if water enters the home.
Keep a stock of dry rations and clean water for at least 72 hours.

Winter.impact: Mild weather, localized agricultural water shortage due to dry winter breeze.
institutional: Manage canal water distribution efficiently for wheat cultivation.
household: Adopt water-saving techniques in domestic and farming activities.

SpringSummer.impact: Extreme heatwaves in Jacobabad, Larkana, and Sukkur; maritime storm risks in Karachi.
institutional: Implement mandatory labor breaks during peak heat hours.
Alert fishermen regarding high sea swell forecasts.
household: Consume plenty of fluids and ORS to maintain hydration.
Secure roofing materials against high-speed coastal winds.

Monsoon.impact: Flash flooding in seasonal dry streams (Nullahs) and mudslide risks in hilly terrains.
institutional: Deploy rescue teams along key communication highways (N-25, N-40).
Clear debris from natural drainage pathways.
household: Do not pitch tents or park vehicles in or near dry riverbeds (nullahs).
Secure emergency communication devices (radio/mobile with backup power).

Winter.impact: Severe cold waves and drought risks in northern districts, snow blockades on passes.
institutional: Pre-position snow-clearing machinery at Lak Pass and Kozak Pass.
Distribute winterization kits to vulnerable communities.
household: Stock fuel wood/gas and dry food items before heavy snow forecast.
Protect exposed water pipes from freezing by wrapping them in insulation.

SpringSummer.impact: Water table depletion and agricultural drought in rain-fed areas.
institutional: Promote water conservation and construct check dams for groundwater recharge.
household: Prioritize water allocation for drinking and livestock over secondary uses.

Monsoon.impact: Landslides in hilly districts (Swat, Kaghan) and flash flooding in Kabul river.
institutional: Establish landslide clearance units along Karakoram Highway and other major routes.
Monitor water levels at Nowshera continuously.
household: Avoid travel during heavy rainfall due to active landslide threats.
Prepare evacuation bags with identity papers, medicines, and cash.

Winter.impact: Heavy snowfall in upper districts, blocking remote valleys.
institutional: Ensure supply chains for essential commodities to cut-off valleys remain open.
household: Keep alternative heating sources ready.
Do not venture out on icy roads without proper snow chains.

SpringSummer.impact: Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOF) in Chitral and flash rainstorms.
institutional: Install GLOF early warning sensors and coordinate community mock drills.
household: Move to higher ground if unusual stream noise or water color change is observed.

Monsoon.impact: GLOF events, landslides blocking the Indus highway, and isolated community valleys.
institutional: Pre-position food reserves and fuel in district headquarters.
Keep helicopter rescue services on standby.
household: Maintain a 15-day food reserve at household level during peak melt/rain season.
Follow local administration instructions immediately if evacuation is ordered.

Winter.impact: Extreme sub-zero temperatures, frostbite risks, and prolonged road blockages.
institutional: Keep district medical centers stocked with frostbite and hypothermia medication.
household: Insulate living spaces and maximize shared heating to conserve fuel.

SpringSummer.impact: High rate of glacial melting leading to sudden valley floods.
institutional: Map safe evacuation paths and update community disaster committees.
household: Avoid building permanent structures near active glacial melt streams.

Monsoon.impact: Landslides, mudslides, and flash floods in Neelum and Jhelum valleys.
institutional: Coordinate with hydroelectric dam operators for controlled water releases.
Set up first aid stations at tourist hotspots.
household: Avoid building near steep slopes or unstable soil banks.
Keep a battery-operated radio tuned to local emergency frequencies.

Winter.impact: Heavy snowfall and sub-zero temperatures in upper Neelum and Leepa valleys.
institutional: Distribute heating fuel subsidies to vulnerable households.
household: Check roof structures for snow weight and clear them regularly.

SpringSummer.impact: Unpredictable hail storms causing damage to fruit orchards.
institutional: Provide crop insurance support and weather alerts for farmers.
household: Use protective netting where possible to safeguard valuable crops.
